items.mistake: Stacking Linear layers without any nonlinear activation between them.
items.fix: Insert ReLU (or another nonlinearity) after each hidden Linear layer. Keep the final output linear for regression or raw logits for cross-entropy.
items.why: Two linear maps compose to one linear map — the stack cannot learn non-linear boundaries like XOR.
items.example:
```python
# Wrong: still one linear function
nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(2, 4), nn.Linear(4, 1))

# Correct: hidden nonlinearity
nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(2, 4), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(4, 1))
```

items.mistake: Using a single-layer model for clearly non-linearly-separable data.
items.fix: Add at least one hidden layer with a nonlinear activation before the output head, then train with an appropriate loss.
items.why: One hyperplane (or logistic linear model) cannot separate patterns such as XOR or concentric rings in 2D.
items.example: XOR labels on four corner points: `Linear(2,1)` stays at ~50% accuracy; `Linear(2,2) → ReLU → Linear(2,1)` can reach 100% with training.

items.mistake: Applying softmax on the hidden layer instead of the output logits.
items.fix: Use ReLU (or tanh/GELU) on hidden tiers; reserve softmax for the final class probabilities at inference (or let CrossEntropyLoss handle logits).
items.why: Softmax on hidden units forces them to compete as a probability simplex, destroying the free feature recombination hidden layers need.
items.example: Replace `nn.Softmax(dim=-1)` after the first hidden Linear with `nn.ReLU()` — hidden activations should not sum to 1 across neurons.
